rows.description: Computus Jacobi Egrembe Rectoris a secundo Die Novembris 1738 ad eundem Diem Revolutionis 1739.
rows.notes: Adopted gemini-flash's transcription for the main header. gpt-5-mini incorrectly split this into two conceptual rows and had several transcription errors ('Decembris' instead of 'Egrembe', 'per ?' instead of 'a secundo', 'Novembris 1739' instead of 'Revolutionis 1739'). gemini-flash's reading is accurate.
rows.section_header: Computus Jacobi Egrembe Rectoris a secundo Die Novembris 1738 ad eundem Diem Revolutionis 1739.

rows.notes: Consensus between extractors for the description and row type.
Consensus on amounts. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Gwinear' as it is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Guinear'.
rows.english_description: Arrears of rent received from the Gwinear estate.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears of rent received from the Gwinear estate, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Visually confirmed gemini-flash's reading for description 'Mic.', shillings '15', and fraction '0.25' (1/4). gpt-5-mini had 'Min', '16', and '0.5', which were incorrect.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Long Wittenham at Michaelmas 1738.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from Long Wittenham at Michaelmas 1738, amounting to £24 15s 2.25d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual arrears received from the South Newington rectory, amounting to £58 0s 0d.

rows.english_description: Arrears received from the Kidlington mills.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received from the Kidlington mills, amounting to £8 16s 11d.

rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Patiol' over gpt-5-mini's 'Calcot', as 'Patiol' aligns better with the visual script. gemini-flash's note acknowledging ambiguity boosted confidence in its careful reading.
rows.english_description: Arrears received from Patiol (alias Lane), a named tenancy or parcel.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears entry for Banister (now Franklyn), amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Adopted amounts from gemini-flash, visually confirmed '10' shillings and '4' pence with no fraction. Overrode description to 'Ladler' based on visual inspection, correcting gemini-flash's 'Lader' and gpt-5-mini's 'Padler'.
rows.english_description: Arrears payment from a tenant or payer named Ladler.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears payment from Ladler, amounting to £4 10s 4d.

rows.notes: Adopted gemini-flash's amounts as gpt-5-mini incorrectly extracted no amounts for this row, despite them being clearly visible in the image ('4 0 0').

rows.notes: Consensus between extractors for the total of the 'Arrearagia recepta' section.

Consensus on amounts. Adopted gpt-5-mini's description 'Q. Thesauro' as it is visually more accurate than gemini-flash's '& Thesauro'.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt recorded from the treasurer, amounting to £32 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus between extractors for the total of the 'Recepta Anni Praesenti' section.
Consensus on row_type. Adopted gemini-flash's description 'Increment. & Batt:' as it includes the period after 'Increment' visible in the image, correcting gpt-5-mini.

Consensus on shillings and pence. Visually confirmed pounds '5' from gemini-flash. Overrode description to 'Increm. ad Fest. O.D.B. 1738' based on close visual inspection, correcting parts from both extractors ('ad' from gpt-5-mini, 'O.D.B.' from visual).

rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment received at the feast of O.D.B. in 1738, amounting to £5 7s 8d.

rows.notes: Consensus on amounts. Combined 'Wist's Battles' from gemini-flash and 'Ter.' from gpt-5-mini, and corrected punctuation to a dash, based on visual verification.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Battels attributable to Wist for specified terms in 1721, amounting to £2 1s 3d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Battels for Bedford for term 1 of 1721, received via Mr Jeale, amounting to £1 10s 7½d.

Visually confirmed pounds '5' from gemini-flash. Adopted gpt-5-mini's description '(mid. 1736)' as it is visually more accurate than gemini-flash's 'O. Mid. 1736' (the 'O.' appears to be a misreading of the parenthesis).

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt to the Commoners' Library dated mid 1736, amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.description: from Mr Liberal Xmas 1736 mid. & Xmas 1737

rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipts for the Commoners' Library at Lady Day and midsummer 1738, amounting to £5 12s 6d.

rows.notes: Overrode both extractors and transcribed directly from the image, which clearly shows '10 5 5 1/2'. This value does not appear to be a direct sum of the preceding entries, but is clearly written as the subtotal.
